programme.title: Home Shopping
programme.desc: Shopping program.

programme.title: ICU
programme.desc: John is admitted to the ICU with serious burns sustained during Victoria's Black Saturday bushfires; 22-year-old Ken is involved in a car crash, which pushes his liver up through his diaphragm. Will these two men survive?

programme.title: ICU
programme.desc: A 19-year-old student fights for her life after being a victim of a car crash; 32-year-old Simon, an epileptic, suffers from a series of serious seizures. Will these patients survive?

programme.title: Weekender
programme.desc: Whether it is daytripping to the reef, watching a fiery outback sunset, or brunch at a cool city cafe, this program will inspire you to make the most of your precious break.

programme.title: South Aussie with Cosi
programme.desc: This week on South Aussie with Cosi, we get excited for Gather Round, explore beautiful Customs House, showcase majestic Kangaroo Island, and celebrate everything SA has to offer.

programme.title: Sydney Weekender
programme.desc: It's a new take on old school darts at Flight Club Sydney. South West Sydney has a new destination for entertainment. From the coast to the hinterland, there's so much to love on the Central Coast.

programme.title: Home Shopping
programme.desc: Shopping program.

programme.title: House of Wellness
programme.desc: Crawf takes on the brutal sport of murderball, Yvie chats with a Pet Doula, Dr Ahmed shares the importance of mole mapping, and Aussie rapper, 360 performs live in studio.

programme.title: Our Town
programme.desc: WA's City of Swan continues to attract people who want to live here, set-up and grow their business, and invest in an area that continues to diversify and thrive.

programme.title: Australia's Big Backyards
programme.desc: Join Australia's much-loved gardening gurus, Jason Hodges and Graham Ross, for a ringside seat to some of Australia's most extraordinary gardens and magical festivals.

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: It's destination Argyll and Bute today, where Sonali Shah tours this region of Scotland in search of a new home for a couple from a Warwickshire town who have a budget of 340,000 pounds.

programme.title: Harry's Practice
programme.desc: Join Dr Harry Cooper and Dr Katrina Warren for everything you need to know about animals.

programme.title: Luxury Escapes: World's Best Holidays
programme.desc: Pia Miranda and Mike Reid explore two extremes of luxury: the raw beauty of Iceland and the timeless elegance of London.

programme.title: Kochie's Business Builders
programme.desc: This week we visit Umina Book Nook on the NSW Central Coast, where Kate has swapped SEO dashboards for shelves of novels. The shop has charm, loyal locals and great books, but Kate needs help turning browsers into buyers.

programme.title: South Aussie with Cosi
programme.desc: This week on South Aussie with Cosi, we get excited for Gather Round, explore beautiful Customs House, showcase majestic Kangaroo Island, and celebrate everything SA has to offer.

programme.title: Better Homes and Gardens
programme.desc: Joh catches up with Craig Leeson to get the lowdown on reducing our plastic usage. Adam builds a unique timber pergola, while Jason makes a vertical feature garden. Ed makes mini banana pancakes.

programme.title: The Yorkshire Vet
programme.desc: In the series' 200th episode, Matt Smith attempts to treat a young deer.

programme.title: Australia's Big Backyards
programme.desc: Join Australia's much-loved gardening gurus, Jason Hodges and Graham Ross, for a ringside seat to some of Australia's most extraordinary gardens and magical festivals.

programme.title: The Aussie Property Flippers
programme.desc: First-time flippers Trevor and Bridie take a punt on a rundown '60s weatherboard on the NSW central coast. Darren Arrandez hopes to turn a three-bedroom shack in Melbourne into a four-bedroom palace. But every day brings more bad news from his tradies, and more pressure on his budget and schedule.

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: It's destination Argyll and Bute today, where Sonali Shah tours this region of Scotland in search of a new home for a couple from a Warwickshire town who have a budget of 340,000 pounds.

programme.title: The Coroner
programme.sub-title: That's the Way To Do It
programme.desc: When the town's new mayor is found dead in a hotel room, Jane's quest for the truth puts her own life in danger.

programme.title: Vera
programme.desc: A suspected poacher is killed by a single gunshot wound, and Vera must look into why he had recently returned to Northumberland after being away for 15 years.

programme.title: Miss Scarlet & the Duke
programme.desc: Eliza receives a bomb in the morning post. Who sent it and why? Eliza teams up with Duke, Moses and her old enemy Patrick Nash to find out who is behind the deadly delivery.

programme.title: The Coroner
programme.sub-title: That's the Way To Do It
programme.desc: When the town's new mayor is found dead in a hotel room, Jane's quest for the truth puts her own life in danger.

programme.title: Harry's Practice
programme.desc: Join Dr Harry Cooper and Dr Katrina Warren for everything you need to know about animals.

programme.title: Better Homes and Gardens
programme.desc: Joh catches up with Craig Leeson to get the lowdown on reducing our plastic usage. Adam builds a unique timber pergola, while Jason makes a vertical feature garden. Ed makes mini banana pancakes.

programme.title: Vera
programme.desc: A suspected poacher is killed by a single gunshot wound, and Vera must look into why he had recently returned to Northumberland after being away for 15 years.

programme.title: Million Dollar Minute
programme.desc: A battle of general knowledge, where every correct answer is a step closer to the ultimate test. Can one person, in one minute, win $1 million?

programme.title: Million Dollar Minute
programme.desc: A battle of general knowledge, where every correct answer is a step closer to the ultimate test. Can one person, in one minute, win $1 million?

programme.title: Surf Patrol
programme.desc: A mother's worst nightmare comes true when her two young children are swept off a sandbank. Then, time is running out for a jet skier who has disappeared in the ocean. And a snapper taking saucy shots on the beach lands in hot water.

programme.title: Our Town
programme.desc: The Kimberley is one Australia's gems, and people can never get enough of this part of WA, whether it's work or play - it continues to amaze. Broome has a lot going on at the moment as well, with plenty of positive stories that are helping this region grow.

programme.title: Last Chance Learners
programme.desc: With only ten days left at the school, some of the learners are still struggling with the basics. Can Sharni take the pressure as she prepares for her driving test?

programme.title: House Calls to the Rescue
programme.desc: The team rescue a couple who have spent six months on a leaky deck and help a gardener who took out a swimming pool and created a titanic disaster.

programme.title: Harry's Practice
programme.desc: Join Dr Harry Cooper and Dr Katrina Warren for everything you need to know about animals.

programme.title: Million Dollar Minute
programme.desc: A battle of general knowledge, where every correct answer is a step closer to the ultimate test. Can one person, in one minute, win $1 million?

programme.title: Million Dollar Minute
programme.desc: A battle of general knowledge, where every correct answer is a step closer to the ultimate test. Can one person, in one minute, win $1 million?

programme.title: Medical Emergency
programme.desc: Kerrie's long-awaited lung operation continues. Will her body reject the donor organ?; a persistent migraine sufferer experiencing photophobia returns for tests and pain management; a father of three in the neurosurgical unit is suffering from a potentially fatal brain tumour. Can he be cured or is it terminal?

programme.title: NBC Today
programme.desc: International news and weather including interviews with newsmakers in the world of politics, business, media, entertainment and sport.

programme.title: Better Homes and Gardens
programme.desc: Joh and Dr Chris head to North Sydney to discover whether it truly offers something for everyone. Charlie shares his top tips for citrus trees. Juliet shares some ideas to add texture to living spaces.

programme.title: Last Chance Learners
programme.desc: With only ten days left at the school, some of the learners are still struggling with the basics. Can Sharni take the pressure as she prepares for her driving test?

programme.title: Better Homes and Gardens
programme.desc: Joh catches up with Craig Leeson to get the lowdown on reducing our plastic usage. Adam builds a unique timber pergola, while Jason makes a vertical feature garden. Ed makes mini banana pancakes.

programme.title: ICU
programme.desc: John is admitted to the ICU with serious burns sustained during Victoria's Black Saturday bushfires; 22-year-old Ken is involved in a car crash, which pushes his liver up through his diaphragm. Will these two men survive?

programme.title: ICU
programme.desc: A 19-year-old student fights for her life after being a victim of a car crash; 32-year-old Simon, an epileptic, suffers from a series of serious seizures. Will these patients survive?

programme.title: Weekender
programme.desc: North Queensland's Townsville region is a diverse and exciting area to explore, from the rugged landscape of Hinchinbrook Island to the beauty of the Great Barrier Reef.

programme.title: Auction Squad
programme.desc: Host Johanna Griggs, with the help of the team - Patrick Thompson, Candy Day, Will Dangar, Luke Dyer and Jason Hodges - transform a classic 1914 Queenslander home into a tropical oasis. The half-demolished bathroom is turned into a fresh, clean practical room with the added bonus of a hidden laundry. The tucked away kitchen is opened up and revamped with new lino and tiles. Faced with a large, overgrown tropical jungle, the squad create two beautiful garden areas.

programme.title: Harry's Practice
programme.desc: Join Dr Harry Cooper and Dr Katrina Warren for everything you need to know about animals.

programme.title: Medical Emergency
programme.desc: Kerrie's long-awaited lung operation continues. Will her body reject the donor organ?; a persistent migraine sufferer experiencing photophobia returns for tests and pain management; a father of three in the neurosurgical unit is suffering from a potentially fatal brain tumour. Can he be cured or is it terminal?

programme.title: Better Homes and Gardens
programme.desc: Joh catches up with Craig Leeson to get the lowdown on reducing our plastic usage. Adam builds a unique timber pergola, while Jason makes a vertical feature garden. Ed makes mini banana pancakes.

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: With a budget of 425,000 pounds to spend, Ginny Buckley is in Norfolk helping retirees make a return to the British countryside after 30 years living in Spain.

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Bargain Hunt is at Newark Showground today, presented by Eric Knowles. Experts Izzie Balmer and Nick Hall guide the teams as they search for items to sell at auction and hopefully make a profit.

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: Martin is appalled to discover that Louisa is moving furniture and even further angered by her decision to interview for her old job as head teacher. Aunty Joan is having money problems, as is Al whose cash-in-hand work for Louisa sets tongues wagging in the village; an elderly couple raise the Doc's suspicions over their motives for needing a disabled car sticker.

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: A rapist, who carries out his sordid crimes in his victims' homes, is the urgent subject of Detective Inspector Jack Frost's enquiries. A young secretary working late is startled by a man hiding in the back of her car. At the same time, a 17-year-old girl is reported missing and a friend of the girl reports seeing a man in her bedroom window. But Frost isn't convinced these incidents are linked to the rapist. Soon afterwards, a 15-year-old girl is raped in the woods, and she quickly points the finger at a mini-cab driver she marked during the struggle. Meanwhile, Frost is invited to London to join a group of heroes who, like him, have been awarded for bravery.

programme.title: Ambulance: Code Red
programme.desc: Follows the work of an ambulance service, giving an insight into the life and death incidents faced by specialist emergency response teams.

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: Martin is appalled to discover that Louisa is moving furniture and even further angered by her decision to interview for her old job as head teacher. Aunty Joan is having money problems, as is Al whose cash-in-hand work for Louisa sets tongues wagging in the village; an elderly couple raise the Doc's suspicions over their motives for needing a disabled car sticker.

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Bargain Hunt is at Newark Showground today, presented by Eric Knowles. Experts Izzie Balmer and Nick Hall guide the teams as they search for items to sell at auction and hopefully make a profit.

programme.title: Harry's Practice
programme.desc: Join Dr Harry Cooper and Dr Katrina Warren for everything you need to know about animals.

programme.title: Medical Emergency
programme.desc: Kerrie's long-awaited lung operation continues. Will her body reject the donor organ?; a persistent migraine sufferer experiencing photophobia returns for tests and pain management; a father of three in the neurosurgical unit is suffering from a potentially fatal brain tumour. Can he be cured or is it terminal?

programme.desc: Joh catches up with Craig Leeson to get the lowdown on reducing our plastic usage. Adam builds a unique timber pergola, while Jason makes a vertical feature garden. Ed makes mini banana pancakes.

programme.desc: A battle of general knowledge, where every correct answer is a step closer to the ultimate test. Can one person, in one minute, win $1 million?
